top_findings.title: TODO comment in use-modules-list.ts
top_findings.description: Forbidden TODO pattern found in production code. Per CLAUDE.md and project rules, TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comments must not be committed.
top_findings.suggestion: Either implement the reactive store approach now, or remove the comment entirely.

top_findings.title: Entire airi-brain service has zero test files
top_findings.description: The services/airi-brain package contains 8 new/modified source files with approximately 800 lines of new business logic and ZERO test files. BrainStore is a critical SQLite-backed persistence layer -- all untested.
top_findings.suggestion: Create services/airi-brain/src/__tests__/store.test.ts with Vitest using real SQLite database.

top_findings.title: Evening pipeline (critical business workflow) completely untested
top_findings.description: runEveningPipeline is a multi-phase critical business workflow with zero tests. Failures would silently corrupt or lose user memory data.
top_findings.suggestion: Create an integration test with real BrainStore and Test Double LLM/embedding providers.

top_findings.title: SSRF via untrusted baseURL in embedding handler proxy
top_findings.description: The embedding handlers accept a baseURL from WebSocket event data and use it directly in server-side fetch calls with the user's API key attached. A malicious client could supply a baseURL pointing to an attacker-controlled server (credential leak) or internal network addresses (SSRF).
top_findings.suggestion: Validate baseURL against an allowlist of known provider domains. At minimum, block private/internal IP ranges and non-HTTPS URLs.

top_findings.title: Empty catch block swallows JSON parse error in embedding validation
top_findings.description: A bare catch block with only a comment silently swallows JSON.parse failure. This violates the project's explicit prohibition on empty catch blocks.
top_findings.suggestion: Bind the error and log at debug level with context.

top_findings.title: Duplicate sendEmbeddingConfig logic in memory and embedding stores
top_findings.description: sendEmbeddingConfig is duplicated nearly identically in both useMemoryModuleStore and useEmbeddingStore. Violates DRY and SRP.
top_findings.suggestion: Remove the embedding config logic from the memory store. Have the memory settings page use the dedicated useEmbeddingStore.

top_findings.title: API keys stored in plaintext in SQLite without encryption
top_findings.description: API keys for LLM, embedding, and provider configs are stored as plaintext in SQLite. If the database file is compromised, all API keys are exposed.
top_findings.suggestion: Encrypt API keys at rest using a key derived from a machine-level secret.

top_findings.title: JSON.parse on DB data without try/catch in BrainStore.getActivitySummary
top_findings.description: getActivitySummary() calls JSON.parse on columns read from SQLite without any try/catch. Corrupted data will crash the caller.
top_findings.suggestion: Wrap in try/catch, returning sensible defaults with a warning log.

top_findings.title: LLM and Embedding adapter HTTP logic untested
top_findings.description: Critical HTTP integration logic: URL construction, Authorization header injection, API request/response parsing, error handling. All untested.
top_findings.suggestion: Create adapters.test.ts. Test URL construction, headers, response parsing, dimension caching, and error handling.

top_findings.title: Hardcoded embedding dimension default (1536) may cause vector DB mismatch
top_findings.description: The embedding adapter starts with hardcoded dimension 1536. If the VectorStore uses this during initialization but the actual model produces different dimensions, this causes data corruption.
top_findings.suggestion: Make the dimension lazy-initialized via a probe embed call, or let consumers handle dimension-unknown state.

positive_observations: Clean vertical slice delivery: LLM and embedding configuration flows end-to-end from settings UI through WebSocket protocol to brain service persistence and context-engine pipeline initialization.
Proper use of @guiiai/logg structured logging throughout all new brain service handlers with consistent field-based context.
Good error handling patterns: all async handlers have try/catch blocks, errors are logged with context, and status is pushed to the frontend.
Protocol events properly typed in plugin-protocol/events.ts with shared interface definitions for both UI and backend consumers.
SQLite persistence for provider configs, LLM config, and embedding config ensures configuration survives restarts -- a significant improvement over previous in-memory-only patterns.
Pipeline rebuild on config change uses debouncing (200ms) to avoid unnecessary reconstructions during rapid edits.
Clean removal of unused gaming modules (Factorio, Minecraft) and memory short/long-term split -- reduces maintenance surface.
Existing Pinia store tests follow good patterns: real Pinia instances via createTestingPinia with stubActions:false, behavioral assertions.
BrainStore uses parameterized SQL queries throughout -- zero string concatenation for SQL, consistent with project best practices.
The provider-sync store implements a clean sync-pause mechanism to avoid echo loops during restore from backend.
Network I/O in adapters.ts consistently checks response.ok and throws with HTTP status + body context.
WebSocket event handlers in embedding.ts and llm.ts properly restore persisted config from DB on startup.
Desktop shell handler has proper .catch() on the promise chain inside setInterval, avoiding the throw-in-timer bug.
The evening-pipeline.ts JSDoc on runEveningPipeline is excellent: numbered list clearly describes the 5-phase pipeline.
Phase comments inside runEveningPipeline are valuable navigation aids in a long async function.
The adapters.ts file has clear JSDoc on both exported functions explaining the adaptation pattern.
Strategy comments in embedding handler explain the fallback logic clearly.
The CORS bypass comments in provider files effectively explain why listModels returns empty array.
Guard clauses and early returns are used effectively throughout.
The pipeline architecture cleanly separates concerns: providers.ts for config, adapters.ts for adaptation, pipeline.ts for orchestration.
Named constants are used for timing values (WINDOW_POLL_INTERVAL_MS, DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_TTL_MS).
Individual functions are generally short and well-scoped -- most under 30 lines.
The server-runtime change to use superjson.parse with proper try/catch and error response is a clean fix.
